description:
You are a cloud-migration architect who has just been handed a comma-separated service inventory that must be reshaped so it can be ingested by a deployment pipeline.  
Your job is to transform the data, generate a per-node summary, and produce an auditable processing log.

Directory layout that already exists:
• /home/user/cloud_migration/source/          (contains the raw input)
• /home/user/cloud_migration/output/          (must hold every artifact you create)
• /home/user/cloud_migration/logs/            (store the processing log here)

Input file that is already present:
• /home/user/cloud_migration/source/services.csv

Required deliverables (all paths are absolute – do NOT change the names):
1. /home/user/cloud_migration/output/active_services.ndjson  
   • New-line-delimited JSON (one JSON object per line, no surrounding array).  
   • Each object MUST have the following keys, in exactly this order:  
     service_name, node, port, protocol, status  
   • Only records whose status field is “active” (case-sensitive match) should appear.  
   • Example of two consecutive lines (whitespace is significant):  
     {"service_name":"auth-api","node":"edge-1","port":443,"protocol":"https","status":"active"}  
     {"service_name":"billing-api","node":"edge-2","port":443,"protocol":"https","status":"active"}  

2. /home/user/cloud_migration/output/node_summary.csv  
   • A header line must be present.  
   • Exactly two columns, in this order: node,count  
   • Each following line contains a node value and the integer count of active services that belong to that node.  
   • Rows may appear in any order, BUT each node listed in the input must appear exactly once if it has at least one active service.  
   • No extra whitespace allowed around commas.  
   • Example (illustrative only):  
     node,count  
     edge-1,2  

3. /home/user/cloud_migration/logs/transform.log  
   • Plain text log capturing each major step you performed.  
   • One line per step, formatted exactly as:  
     ISO-8601-timestamp   STEP_NAME   RECORDS_IN   RECORDS_OUT  
     (Fields are separated by a single TAB character, not spaces.)  
   • Example of a well-formed line (do not use this content literally):  
     2024-04-03T15:41:17Z	FilterActive	7	5  
   • At minimum, the log must contain lines for: reading the CSV, filtering active records, writing the NDJSON, generating the summary. You can add more if you wish, but the mandatory ones must exist and reflect the real numbers you processed.  

Additional requirements:
• Perform all manipulations using standard Linux command-line tooling available in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu environment (e.g., awk, sed, grep, cut, parallel, jq, etc.).  
• Because the data volume can be large in real scenarios, do not rely on single-threaded processing only. Use GNU Parallel or an equivalent to illustrate multi-step parallel execution at least once (e.g., parallelizing the JSON conversion).  
• Preserve the original input file; do not modify or overwrite anything under /home/user/cloud_migration/source/.  
• Place every artifact you create strictly inside the designated output or logs directories.  
• Once done, you should be able to cat both result files and see well-formatted outputs matching the rules above.  

The automated tests will:
1. Verify that the NDJSON file exists, is not empty, and every line is valid JSON with exactly the expected keys and only active services.  
2. Check that node_summary.csv has the correct header and accurate counts that sum up to the total number of lines in active_services.ndjson.  
3. Inspect transform.log to ensure the required step names are present and that numerical fields are correct and internally consistent.  

Work entirely from the Linux terminal. No GUI tools. Show command output along the way so a human reviewer can follow your reasoning.
truth:
Initial filesystem items present before the agent starts:

1. File: /home/user/cloud_migration/source/services.csv  
Exact content (7 lines + header):  
service_name,node,port,protocol,status  
auth-api,edge-1,443,https,active  
billing-api,edge-2,443,https,active  
legacy-report,node-5,8080,http,inactive  
user-db,edge-1,5432,postgres,active  
cache,node-3,6379,redis,maintenance  
frontend,node-2,80,http,active  
search,node-2,9200,http,active  

Expected artifacts after successful completion:

A. /home/user/cloud_migration/output/active_services.ndjson  
Exact 5-line content (newline at end of every line):  
{"service_name":"auth-api","node":"edge-1","port":443,"protocol":"https","status":"active"}  
{"service_name":"billing-api","node":"edge-2","port":443,"protocol":"https","status":"active"}  
{"service_name":"user-db","node":"edge-1","port":5432,"protocol":"postgres","status":"active"}  
{"service_name":"frontend","node":"node-2","port":80,"protocol":"http","status":"active"}  
{"service_name":"search","node":"node-2","port":9200,"protocol":"http","status":"active"}  

B. /home/user/cloud_migration/output/node_summary.csv  
Exact 4-line content (trailing newline at each line):  
node,count  
edge-1,2  
edge-2,1  
node-2,2  

C. /home/user/cloud_migration/logs/transform.log  
Must contain at least the four lines below (the timestamp can differ, but STEP_NAME and counts must match). Tabs separate fields; shown here with ⟶ symbol for clarity (the symbol is NOT part of the file):  
YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ⟶ReadCSV⟶0⟶7  
YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ⟶FilterActive⟶7⟶5  
YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ⟶WriteNDJSON⟶5⟶5  
YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ⟶GenerateSummary⟶5⟶3  

Where:  
• The first numeric field after each step is RECORDS_IN; the second is RECORDS_OUT.  
• Any additional lines the agent chooses to add must not contradict these numbers.

File permissions: the pre-existing directories have standard user read/write/execute (0755) and the CSV file is 0644. The agent has write permission inside /home/user/cloud_migration/output/ and /home/user/cloud_migration/logs/.

No other hidden requirements exist.
